decisions.decision: Use binary (Yes/No) outcomes for all probability boundary tests (0, 0.5, 1). Add one multi-outcome test for color cycling coverage since it's low cost.
alternatives_rejected: Only binary outcomes with no multi-outcome test

decisions.decision: Do not duplicate edge case tests (empty outcomes, missing marginals) since they already exist in ui-components.test.tsx.
alternatives_rejected: Duplicate all edge case tests in the new file for standalone completeness

decisions.decision: Assert both visible span text and title attributes for percentage display. Assert inline style width property for bar width styling.
Place test file at frontend/src/__tests__/ProbabilityBar.test.tsx using vitest + @testing-library/react with the existing jsdom setup.

steps.task: Create frontend/src/__tests__/ProbabilityBar.test.tsx with a describe block containing tests: (a) renders with probability 0 — both outcomes at 0, verify 0.0% text, 0% width, minWidth 0; (b) renders with probability 0.5 — both outcomes at 0.5, verify 50.0% text, 50% width, minWidth 2; (c) renders with probability 1 — one outcome at 1 and other at 0, verify 100.0% and 0.0% text, 100% and 0% widths; (d) displays percentage text in both title attributes and visible span labels; (e) correct bar width styling via inline style assertions; (f) multi-outcome test with 3+ outcomes verifying color cycling wraps at OUTCOME_COLORS.length.
Run vitest on the new test file to verify all tests pass: npx vitest run frontend/src/__tests__/ProbabilityBar.test.tsx
Run the full test suite to verify no regressions: npx vitest run
